question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the endpoint GET /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens with high-concurrency requests, each triggering EntityService.getEncodedId() plus a full token_allowance DB query, exhausting the database connection pool and causing the mirror node to stop processing new ingested transactions, resulting in total service unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with both spender.id range filters (gte + lte) and token.id range filters (gte + lte) to force extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to produce a three-part UNION ALL query (lower + inner + upper subqueries), each independently fetching up to limit rows from token_allowance, multiplying DB load by 3x per request and causing DB connection pool exhaustion under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests using a valid-format but non-existent account ID (e.g., 0.0.999999999) as the path parameter, causing getAccountTokenAllowances() to skip any account existence check (unlike tokenController.js which calls EntityService.isValidAccount()), execute a full token_allowance table scan with owner=$1 condition, and under high concurrency exhaust DB resources causing network-wide transaction confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address as the path parameter (idOrAliasOrEvmAddress), forcing EntityService.getEncodedId() to execute a DB lookup via entityFromEvmAddressQuery before the main token_allowance query, doubling the number of DB queries per request and under sustained concurrent load exhausting the DB connection pool to the point where the importer cannot write new transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a base32 account alias as the path parameter, forcing EntityService.getAccountIdFromAlias() to execute entityFromAliasQuery against the entity table, and by sending thousands of concurrent requests with non-existent aliases cause repeated DB queries that saturate the connection pool and prevent the mirror node from confirming new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit to the maximum allowed value (100) and combine it with a spender.id+token.id range filter combination that generates 3 UNION ALL subqueries, causing each request to fetch up to 300 rows from token_allowance before the outer limit is applied, and under concurrent load cause memory and DB resource exhaustion that halts transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: TokenAllowanceService.getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the three-subquery UNION ALL path in getSubQuery() by providing spender.id=gte:1&spender.id=lte:999999999&token.id=gte:1&token.id=lte:999999999, causing each subquery to perform a broad range scan on the token_allowance table with no selective index, resulting in full or near-full table scans that lock DB resources and prevent transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any rate limiting at the controller level in getAccountTokenAllowances() to send thousands of requests per second, each consuming a DB connection for the duration of the query, exhausting the connection pool and causing the mirror node importer to fail to write new consensus transactions to the database?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with spender.id=gte:1 and token.id=gte:1 (lower bound on both primary and secondary), causing extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to produce a lower+inner two-part UNION ALL, and by flooding with concurrent such requests cause sustained DB overload that prevents new transaction records from being confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getAccountTokenAllowances() with an account that has a very large number of token allowances, causing the select * from token_allowance query to return the maximum limit rows, each row containing all columns, and under concurrent load cause memory exhaustion in the Node.js process that crashes the REST API server and halts transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getAccountTokenAllowances() uses select * (all columns) from token_allowance rather than selecting specific columns, causing unnecessarily large result payloads per query, and under high concurrency exhaust Node.js heap memory causing process crash and network transaction confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests that each produce the maximum three-subquery UNION ALL (lower+inner+upper) by providing spender.id=gte:X&spender.id=lte:Y&token.id=gte:A&token.id=lte:B, causing the DB to execute 3 sequential subqueries per request, and under load cause the DB statement timeout to be reached for legitimate importer writes, halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a valid EVM address path parameter that does not exist in the entity table, causing EntityService.getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to execute entityFromEvmAddressQuery and return empty, then throw NotFoundError, but under high concurrency the repeated DB queries for non-existent EVM addresses saturate the DB connection pool and prevent transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: TokenAllowanceService.getQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that produces the three-subquery UNION ALL path in getQuery(), where each subquery shares the same params array (params is mutated by each getSubQuery() call via params.push()), causing the outer UNION ALL query to have a very large params array, and under concurrent load cause excessive memory allocation in the query builder that exhausts Node.js heap?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of IP-based or session-based rate limiting on the /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens endpoint to send sustained high-volume requests from multiple source IPs (distributed attack), causing the DB connection pool to be fully occupied by token allowance queries and preventing the importer from acquiring connections to write new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with only token.id filter (no spender.id), which is rejected by validateSecondaryBound() with InvalidArgumentError, but by sending thousands of such requests per second cause the error handling path to consume CPU and event loop capacity, degrading the mirror node's ability to process new transaction confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with the maximum allowed limit parameter value (100) and no filter parameters, causing getAccountTokenAllowances() to execute a full owner-scoped scan of token_allowance returning 100 rows per request, and under high concurrency cause DB I/O saturation that prevents the importer from writing new consensus transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: TokenAllowanceService.getSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the inner subquery path in getSubQuery() by providing spender.id range filters, causing the inner subquery to use a broad spender range (e.g., spender > 1) with no upper bound on token_id, resulting in a near-full table scan of token_allowance that holds a DB connection for an extended period and under concurrency exhausts the pool?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all valid account IDs (0.0.1 through 0.0.N) and send parallel requests for each, causing getAccountTokenAllowances() to execute one DB query per account ID with no caching layer at the controller level, generating a sustained DB query storm that exhausts connections and halts transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with spender.id=gte:1 (lower bound only, no upper) and token.id=gte:1 (lower bound only), causing extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to produce a two-part UNION ALL (lower+inner), where the inner subquery has spender > 1 with no upper bound, performing a near-full table scan, and under concurrency cause DB resource exhaustion preventing transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the async nature of getAccountTokenAllowances() — which awaits EntityService.getEncodedId() and then awaits TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() sequentially — to hold two DB connections per request (one for entity lookup, one for allowance query) under concurrent load, doubling the connection pool pressure and causing network transaction confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests targeting an account with a large number of token allowances (e.g., 10,000 rows in token_allowance), causing getAccountTokenAllowances() to return the full limit (100) rows per request, and by sending concurrent requests cause the DB to perform repeated expensive range scans on the token_allowance table that starve the importer of DB resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a crafted alias string that passes AccountAlias.isValid() but does not exist in the entity table, causing EntityService.getAccountIdFromAlias() to execute a DB query and throw NotFoundError, and by sending thousands of such requests per second cause repeated DB queries that saturate the connection pool and prevent transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with spender.id=lte:999999999 and token.id=lte:999999999 (upper bounds only), causing extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to produce an inner+upper two-part UNION ALL where the inner subquery has spender < 999999999 with no lower bound, performing a near-full table scan, and under concurrency exhaust DB connections preventing transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the token allowance endpoint performs no account existence validation (unlike tokenController.js which calls EntityService.isValidAccount()) to send requests for millions of non-existent account IDs, each triggering a token_allowance table query that returns empty results but still consumes a DB connection, causing connection pool exhaustion and halting transaction confirmation?
